title: The Gang Beats Boggs: Ladies Reboot

jokes.text: Wade Boggs. He's a Hall of Fame baseball guy who drank 70 beers on a cross-country flight. We're gonna beat that record.
jokes.setup: Character announces they're going to beat a drinking record
jokes.punchline: The casual way she frames an impossible drinking feat as achievable
jokes.explanation: Sets up the entire episode's premise with characteristic Sunny confidence in a terrible plan

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Wade Boggs. He's a Hall of Fame baseball guy who drank 70 beers on a cross-country flight. We're gonna beat that record.

jokes.text: I didn't know it was an all-female flight. That feels dangerous.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casually sexist assumption delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie ignorance delivered with characteristic obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I didn't know it was an all-female flight. That feels dangerous.

jokes.text: Can't smoke on an airplane. Goddamn, when's the last time you flew?
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to smoke on plane
jokes.punchline: Mac's exasperated response to Charlie's complete ignorance of basic flight rules
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing Charlie's disconnect from reality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Can't smoke on an airplane. Goddamn, when's the last time you flew?

jokes.text: It's the same exact thing, but it's with women. Right? So it's a new idea.
jokes.setup: Defending the concept of repeating the men's challenge
jokes.punchline: The illogical reasoning that gender-swapping equals innovation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on lazy reboots disguised as character stupidity

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: It's the same exact thing, but it's with women. Right? So it's a new idea.

jokes.text: Like some badass woman who could really throw them back? You know, Judy Garland, Liz Taylor... Joan Crawford... Betty Ford!
jokes.setup: Looking for female drinking role models
jokes.punchline: List escalates from entertainers to former First Lady, all with serious addiction issues
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that builds to an absurd climax with Betty Ford

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Like some badass woman who could really throw them back? You know, Judy Garland, Liz Taylor... Joan Crawford... Betty Ford!

jokes.text: Those are all sad examples of alcohol abuse. The Boggs thing just feels more fun, you know? When women do it, it just feels sad.
jokes.setup: Rejecting the female alcoholic role models
jokes.punchline: The casual misogyny in assuming women drinking is inherently sadder
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on double standards delivered through character obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Those are all sad examples of alcohol abuse. The Boggs thing just feels more fun, you know? When women do it, it just feels sad.

jokes.text: Last time, Mac was commissioner, but I figured I could do that and participate because women are excellent multitaskers.
jokes.setup: Explaining role change from previous episode
jokes.punchline: Using a sexist stereotype to justify taking on more work
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how the characters internalize and weaponize stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Last time, Mac was commissioner, but I figured I could do that and participate because women are excellent multitaskers.

jokes.text: How come the coloreds are allowed to sit up front with the whites and we're way back here?
jokes.punchline: Casually racist comment delivered with complete obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Mrs. Mac's casual bigotry

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: How come the coloreds are allowed to sit up front with the whites and we're way back here?

jokes.text: Wow. I never heard you talk so much. Truly awful.
jokes.punchline: Dee's matter-of-fact response to the awfulness
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction that acknowledges the horror while moving on

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Wow. I never heard you talk so much. Truly awful.

jokes.text: She ate all her nicotine patches already. Ate them?! What? You're not supposed to eat them.
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she ate nicotine patches and Mac's incredulous response
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation with perfect character logic

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She ate all her nicotine patches already.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Ate them?! What? You're not supposed to eat them.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Two women pilots. I'm getting out of here.
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Mac's immediate panic and attempt to exit mid-flight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mrs. Mac panic response with absurd attempted solution

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, no. Two women pilots. I'm getting out of here.

jokes.text: We're in mid-goddamn-air. You know what? Drink your beer. That'll calm your nerves.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Mac trying to escape the plane
jokes.punchline: Dee's practical response treating alcohol as anxiety medication
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing Dee's priorities

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We're in mid-goddamn-air. You know what? Drink your beer. That'll calm your nerves.

jokes.text: Why do women pee so much?
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confused sexist observation
jokes.explanation: Basic Charlie ignorance, functional but unremarkable

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why do women pee so much?

jokes.text: I'm an alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Asked about her record chugging secret
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission of alcoholism as expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy with economy of words and character truth

dialogue.speaker: The Waitress
dialogue.line: I'm an alcoholic.

jokes.text: Secretariat? The racehorse?
jokes.punchline: The reveal that she's thinking of a horse, not a person
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with perfect Artemis logic

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Secretariat? The racehorse?

jokes.text: I don't like that she was named after a secretary. She should be a boss.
jokes.punchline: Artemis misunderstanding the word 'secretariat' as relating to secretaries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis pseudo-feminist misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: I don't like that she was named after a secretary. She should be a boss.

jokes.text: I don't get into this bathroom soon I'm gonna shit my pants!
jokes.punchline: Charlie's crude, desperate announcement
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor, saved by character integration

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I don't get into this bathroom soon I'm gonna shit my pants!

jokes.text: Yes! Do it! See? That's what I'm talking about. Gross-out humor. It's like taking the original to the next level. Nobody expects that from women. It's so much more shocking, isn't it?
jokes.punchline: Dee's meta-commentary on gendered comedy expectations
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary disguised as character enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yes! Do it! See? That's what I'm talking about. Gross-out humor. It's like taking the original to the next level. Nobody expects that from women. It's so much more shocking, isn't it?

jokes.text: They don't check medical bags. See? The guys didn't do that last time. How's that for originality?
jokes.punchline: Her pride in this 'innovation' while missing the obvious illegality
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Dee's criminal innovation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: They don't check medical bags. See? The guys didn't do that last time. How's that for originality?

jokes.text: Just, uh, crystals, worry dolls, dream catchers, labia rings... your basics.
jokes.punchline: The casual inclusion of 'labia rings' in the list of basics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis escalation with shocking casualness

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: Just, uh, crystals, worry dolls, dream catchers, labia rings... your basics.

jokes.text: It's a land of sad, lonely, desperate women willing to pay any price for fake spirituality and clean orgasms.
jokes.setup: Explaining her business plan for California
jokes.punchline: The cynical yet accurate description of her target market
jokes.explanation: Sharp social satire delivered through perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: It's a land of sad, lonely, desperate women willing to pay any price for fake spirituality and clean orgasms.

jokes.text: Ooh, I want to... I-I want to 'gasm.
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Mac's awkward, childlike interest
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable character moment that works for Mrs. Mac

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: Ooh, I want to... I-I want to 'gasm.

jokes.text: How do I keep winding up with you people?
jokes.punchline: His existential despair at repeated encounters
jokes.explanation: Good callback establishing this character's ongoing misfortune

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: How do I keep winding up with you people?

jokes.text: How come you're a man servant? Shouldn't there be a woman servant on an all-female flight?
jokes.punchline: Dee's backwards logic about gender segregation
jokes.explanation: Solid character logic that shows her warped thinking

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: How come you're a man servant? Shouldn't there be a woman servant on an all-female flight?

jokes.text: Great. You're a soy boy beta cuck.
jokes.setup: Flight attendant explains his feminist motivation
jokes.punchline: Dee's dismissive use of internet insult terminology
jokes.explanation: Topical humor that works for the character

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Great. You're a soy boy beta cuck.

jokes.text: And you can put it on the Boggs account. Just make sure you keep all the ladies in the white tees lubricated.
jokes.punchline: The nonexistent 'Boggs account' and crude phrasing about keeping people drunk
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist confidence combined with crude objectification

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: And you can put it on the Boggs account. Just make sure you keep all the ladies in the white tees lubricated.

jokes.text: Uh, in honor of the Women's March, we are serving pink wine. So we only have rosé.
jokes.punchline: The airline's patronizing pink wine choice for women
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate pandering to women

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: Uh, in honor of the Women's March, we are serving pink wine. So we only have rosé.

jokes.text: I just found out Secretariat was a boy. All racehorses are male. I know, because I find them so attractive.
jokes.punchline: His admission of attraction to horses as proof of their maleness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - disturbing personal revelation delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I just found out Secretariat was a boy. All racehorses are male. I know, because I find them so attractive.

jokes.text: Pussies on the track.
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Mac's crude but technically accurate phrasing
jokes.explanation: Economical wordplay that fits Mrs. Mac's bluntness

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: Pussies on the track.

jokes.text: I happen to know that tennis great Martina Navratilova drank 70 beers on a flight to L.A., only to wake up the next morning and beat her rival, Chris Evert, at the California Open.
jokes.setup: Need for a female athlete inspiration
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated story mixing real tennis players with impossible drinking feat
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist fabrication with perfect confidence and detail

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I happen to know that tennis great Martina Navratilova drank 70 beers on a flight to L.A., only to wake up the next morning and beat her rival, Chris Evert, at the California Open.

jokes.text: So now we're gonna try to beat Martina Tinatinova...
jokes.setup: Trying to remember the made-up story
jokes.punchline: Immediately mangling the name she just said
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Dee's inability to maintain her own lies

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: So now we're gonna try to beat Martina Tinatinova...

jokes.text: You can't just change one small specific and call it new. I changed three! The wine... the athlete, and the bag!
jokes.punchline: Dee's desperate defense counting minimal changes
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on lazy remake culture through character argument

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: You can't just change one small specific and call it new.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I changed three! The wine... the athlete, and the bag!

jokes.text: Oh, dear! Watching a woman do math scares me!
jokes.punchline: Charlie's over-the-top sexist reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard Charlie sexism, functional but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, dear! Watching a woman do math scares me!

jokes.text: Man, it is sad that there are only chicks on this plane 'cause, I mean, I am all horned up.
jokes.punchline: His crude expression of sexual frustration despite being closeted
jokes.explanation: Standard Mac denial about his sexuality

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Man, it is sad that there are only chicks on this plane 'cause, I mean, I am all horned up.

jokes.text: Well, there is a-a male servant. Oh, yeah? Hot? No. Soy boy beta cuck.
jokes.punchline: Dee's dismissal using her earlier insult
jokes.explanation: Functional callback that maintains character voice

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, there is a-a male servant.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah? Hot?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Soy boy beta cuck.

jokes.text: The only way to beat men is by competing against other women.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing internalized misogyny
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about competitive dynamics disguised as character stupidity

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: The only way to beat men is by competing against other women.

jokes.text: And the goddess stone is for putting up your snatch.
jokes.punchline: Casual description of intimate use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis bluntness about sexual products

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: And the goddess stone is for putting up your snatch.

jokes.text: No, it's my special 'uni-tea.' Well, it's selling like hotcakes. It helps your prostate.

jokes.explanation: Weak pun but good character scamming

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: No, it's my special 'uni-tea.' Well, it's selling like hotcakes. It helps your prostate.

jokes.text: Women don't have a prostate, goddammit. Or is that what men want you to believe?
jokes.punchline: Artemis turning basic biology into conspiracy theory

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Women don't have a prostate, goddammit.

jokes.text: This Boggs is busted, 'cause I'm doing my own thing now. And it's empowering and communal. Next level shit, right, ladies?
jokes.punchline: Describing her scam as empowering while taking people's money
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how exploitation gets reframed as empowerment

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: This Boggs is busted, 'cause I'm doing my own thing now. And it's empowering and communal. Next level shit, right, ladies?

jokes.text: Yeah, six is a yonic number, I'm cool with it.
jokes.setup: Being called out for only having six drinks
jokes.punchline: Using fake spiritual terminology to justify low performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis nonsense that sounds convincing while being meaningless

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: Yeah, six is a yonic number, I'm cool with it.

jokes.text: I'm posting up. I figure a plane full of broads, there's gonna be a lot of horny women, so I'm like a spider waiting back here to catch some flies.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his presence on the flight
jokes.punchline: His predatory metaphor for picking up women
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank grossness with vivid predatory imagery

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm posting up. I figure a plane full of broads, there's gonna be a lot of horny women, so I'm like a spider waiting back here to catch some flies.

jokes.text: In every reboot you gotta have somebody from the original to make a cameo.
jokes.punchline: Frank's meta-explanation using reboot terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on reboot culture delivered perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: In every reboot you gotta have somebody from the original to make a cameo.

jokes.text: Want to rumble? No, I don't, but I do want to beat Dennis, so just pop it in.

dialogue.line: Want to rumble?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, I don't, but I do want to beat Dennis, so just pop it in.

jokes.text: Oh, you better say cheese, buddy, 'cause you're about to go viral.

dialogue.line: Oh, you better say cheese, buddy, 'cause you're about to go viral.

dialogue.line: Heard you were doing the Boggs Soggs lady reboot thing. Did you lose my invitation?

jokes.text: I had sex in the bathroom with Frank, and now I'm in a shame spiral. I'm gonna drink myself to death.

jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing the dark consequences of the competition

jokes.text: Martina Navratilova? I never thought you'd give up when you're so close to beating the record.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Martina Navratilova? I never thought you'd give up when you're so close to beating the record.

jokes.text: Now you're imagining me as Lori Petty in A League of Their Own. That was a good female reboot. No, it was its own thing.
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on good vs bad female-centered movies
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on the difference between reboots and original stories

dialogue.speaker: Hallucination Martina
dialogue.line: Now you're imagining me as Lori Petty in A League of Their Own. That was a good female reboot. No, it was its own thing.

jokes.text: I never drank 70 beers before winning the California Open. That's not even a real event.

jokes.explanation: Perfect deconstruction of the lie that started the new plan

dialogue.speaker: Martina
dialogue.line: I never drank 70 beers before winning the California Open. That's not even a real event.

jokes.text: I've been playing the game the whole time. You're one behind me, loser.

jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Gail's been ahead all along

dialogue.speaker: Gail
dialogue.line: I've been playing the game the whole time. You're one behind me, loser.

jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal that recontextualizes earlier scenes

dialogue.line: That tea that I served was spiked with ayahuasca.

jokes.text: It's coming out both ends. The maximum gross-out. So nobody saw that coming from women.

jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier meta-discussion about gendered comedy expectations

dialogue.speaker: Artemis
dialogue.line: It's coming out both ends. The maximum gross-out. So nobody saw that coming from women.

dialogue.line: We're in North Dakota. And only those passengers deemed fit to fly will go on to Los Angeles. And you're not one of them.
